entities.name: TOMATO
entities.description: A widely grown vegetable (Lycopersicon esculentum) susceptible to vascular wilt disease.

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. LYCOPERSICI
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that is the causal agent of vascular wilt in tomato plants.

entities.name: VASCULAR WILT
entities.description: A disease characterized by the colonization of vascular tissue, leading to plant wilting and death.

entities.name: WILTING
entities.description: A physical symptom of vascular wilt involving the collapse and death of the plant.

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: Tools used for the detection of FOL and the screening of tomato races.

entities.name: GENOMIC APPROACHES
entities.description: Analytical tools used to identify informative candidates involved in pathogenicity.

entities.name: PROTEOMIC APPROACHES
entities.description: Analytical tools used to identify informative candidates involved in pathogenicity.

entities.name: PATHOGENICITY
entities.description: The ability of a pathogen to cause disease in a host.

entities.name: VIRULENCE
entities.description: The degree of pathogenicity or the severity of the disease caused by the pathogen.

entities.name: MYCOTOXIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: A physiological activity of the fungus that contributes to the wilting of the host plant.

entities.name: INACTIVATION OF HOST DEFENSE
entities.description: A physiological activity of the fungus that suppresses the host's immune response to facilitate infection.

entities.name: PRODUCTION OF TYLOSES
entities.description: A physiological response or activity contributing to the blockage of xylem and subsequent wilting.
